scenarios.title: PACS JVM Auto-Recovery
scenarios.subtitle: INC0002101 · IMPAX/EI App Server · 6 hospital sites impacted

steps.label: Alert Correlation
steps.summary: 186 Prometheus alerts correlated into 1 incident — INC0002101 raised

events.msg: Ingesting Prometheus alert stream — heap_used_ratio > 0.95
186 duplicate alerts detected — deduplicating by CI fingerprint
INC0002101 raised — P1 · IMPAX/EI App Server · 6 sites impacted

steps.label: Root Cause Analysis
steps.summary: DICOM receiver thread pool heap leak identified — 91% confidence · matches INC0002065

events.msg: Traversing PACS dependency graph — mapping blast radius
Correlating JVM heap timeline vs DICOM receiver thread allocation
Vector DB match: INC0002065 — 94% similarity · same heap leak pattern
Root cause confirmed: DICOM receiver thread pool leak · confidence 91%

steps.label: Heap Dump Capture
steps.summary: 2.1GB heap dump saved — forensic evidence preserved before restart

events.msg: Risk score: 18/100 — Sentinel threshold clear · auto-execution approved
kubectl exec pacs-app-prod-01 -- jmap -dump:format=b,file=/tmp/heap.hprof
Heap dump 2.1GB saved — INC0002101-heap.hprof

steps.label: Rolling Restart
steps.summary: Zero-downtime rolling restart — all 3 pods recovered · heap 98% → 12%

events.msg: Rolling restart initiated — one pod at a time · zero-downtime strategy
Pod 1/3: heap 11% ✓ | CPU 24% ✓ | DICOM receiver healthy ✓
Pod 2/3: heap 13% ✓ | CPU 26% ✓ | DICOM receiver healthy ✓
Pod 3/3: heap 12% ✓ | CPU 22% ✓ | DICOM receiver healthy ✓

steps.label: JVM Config Patch
steps.summary: JVM heap increased -Xmx8g → -Xmx12g · prevents recurrence while code fix scheduled

events.msg: Applying permanent config: -Xmx12g via ConfigMap patch
ConfigMap updated · rolling restart triggered · all pods applying new config

steps.label: Validation & Close
steps.summary: All 6 hospital sites restored · MTTR 18m 22s · INC0002101 auto-closed in ServiceNow

events.msg: Health check: all 6 hospital sites — DICOM retrieval restored
MTTR: 18m 22s — P1 SLA MET (target 4h)
INC0002101 auto-closed in ServiceNow · KB-AG-001 updated

scenarios.blastRadius: 6 hospital sites — DICOM retrieval, radiology workflow, reporting

outcome.result: PACS fully restored — all 6 hospital sites operational

scenarios.title: HL7 Gateway Message Recovery
scenarios.subtitle: INC0002098 · 2,847 messages · patient admission flow blocked

steps.label: Alert Correlation
steps.summary: HL7 consumer thread deadlock detected — INC0002098 raised · 2,847 messages backing up

events.msg: Datadog alert: hl7_queue_depth > 2000 · consumer threads not processing
INC0002098 raised — P1 · HL7 Gateway · patient admission flow blocked
Blast radius: all hospital ADT/ORM flows — patient data blocked

steps.label: Root Cause Analysis
steps.summary: Consumer thread deadlock confirmed — same pattern as INC0002065 resolved this morning

events.msg: Analysing HL7 thread dump — checking for deadlock signatures
Thread deadlock confirmed — threads waiting on DB connection pool lock
Matches INC0002065 pattern — risk score 34 · HiTL required for gateway restart

steps.label: Human Approval
steps.summary: Sofia Reyes approved gateway restart — 11 second approval time

events.msg: Risk score 34 — above Sentinel threshold · HiTL approval required
ServiceNow approval task sent to Sofia Reyes (AIOps Engineer)
✓ APPROVED by Sofia Reyes — 11 seconds · gateway restart authorised

steps.label: Gateway Restart & Queue Replay
steps.summary: HL7 gateway restarted · 2,847 messages replayed · patient admission flow restored

events.msg: HL7 Gateway restart initiated — consumer thread pool cleared
Gateway healthy · consumer threads active · processing 340 msg/min
Queue replay complete — 2,847 messages delivered · 0 lost

steps.label: Validation & Close
steps.summary: All hospital ADT/ORM flows restored · INC0002098 closed in ServiceNow

events.msg: HL7 success rate: 99.94% — within SLA · queue depth 0
INC0002098 auto-closed · MTTR: 24m 18s · KB-AG-002 updated

scenarios.blastRadius: All hospital ADT/ORM flows — patient admission data blocked
scenarios.reversibility: HIGH — gateway restart, queue replay, fully reversible

outcome.result: 2,847 HL7 messages replayed — patient flows restored

scenarios.whyHitl: HL7 gateway restart affects live patient ADT and ORM message flows across all hospital sites. While ZeroOps has confirmed the root cause and prepared the restart plan, a service interruption to patient data routing requires clinical operations sign-off before execution. Risk score 34 — above the autonomous threshold of 25.

remediationPlan.act: Confirm thread deadlock — jstack analysis on HL7 gateway pod
Human approval — patient data flow impact acknowledged
Rolling restart of HL7 gateway pod — zero message loss strategy
Replay 2,847 queued messages from dead-letter queue in order
Validate HL7 success rate > 99.9% across all hospital sites
Close INC0002098 in ServiceNow — attach replay confirmation

scenarios.title: Problem Management — PACS Recurring Heap Leak
scenarios.subtitle: PRB-AG-00018 · INC0002101 + INC0002065 · permanent code fix initiated

steps.label: Pattern Detection
steps.summary: 2 PACS JVM heap incidents in 18 hours detected — recurring pattern confirmed

events.msg: Querying incident history: PACS JVM heap · last 30 days
Pattern confirmed: INC0002065 (08:15) + INC0002101 (02:47) — same DICOM receiver leak
Root cause: object reference leak in DicomReceiverThread.java:412 · code fix required

steps.label: Problem Record Raised
steps.summary: PRB-AG-00018 raised in ServiceNow — both incidents linked · full diagnostic bundle attached

events.msg: PRB-AG-00018 created in ServiceNow · linked: INC0002101, INC0002065
Diagnostic bundle attached: heap dumps, thread traces, RCA report

steps.label: Fix Options
steps.summary: Problem Manager presented 3 options — Option 2 selected: permanent code fix + interim heap increase

events.msg: Option 1: Increase heap to 16g (£0 cost, buys 4 weeks) — interim only
Option 2: Permanent code fix DicomReceiverThread.java + heap to 12g (2 dev days)
Option 3: Replace DICOM receiver with vendor patch (4 weeks, £12K)
✓ APPROVED: Option 2 — permanent code fix selected by Dr. Stefan Müller

steps.label: Handoff to CI/CD Pipeline
steps.summary: Fix requirement handed to Jenkins pipeline — control passed to AGFA release management

events.msg: ServiceNow change CHG-8821 created — DicomReceiverThread.java object lifecycle fix
Jenkins pipeline triggered — build CHG-8821-fix queued in AGFA release management
⚡ Control passed to customer release pipeline — ZeroOps monitoring handoff complete

steps.label: KEDB & Monitoring
steps.summary: KEDB updated · monitoring window active · PRB-AG-00018 in controlled resolution

events.msg: KEDB updated — DicomReceiverThread heap leak pattern documented
30-day monitoring window active — auto-alert if pattern recurs before fix deployed

scenarios.blastRadius: Recurring PACS outages — 6 hospital sites each occurrence
scenarios.reversibility: HIGH — code fix via CI/CD, interim config patch applied
scenarios.slaStatus: Problem record raised · code fix in pipeline · pattern eliminated

outcome.result: PRB-AG-00018 raised · CHG-8821 handed to AI SDLC pipeline

scenarios.whyHitl: Raising a Problem record and initiating a code fix pipeline requires confirmation that the recurring pattern justifies permanent engineering effort. The decision to allocate development resource and trigger the AI SDLC pipeline requires a senior engineer or change manager to confirm. ZeroOps has prepared full evidence — two P1 incidents in 18 hours — for the decision.

remediationPlan.act: Correlate INC0002101 and INC0002065 — confirm recurring pattern
Human sign-off — confirm PRB-AG-00018 warrants permanent code fix
Raise PRB-AG-00018 in ServiceNow — link both incidents
Trigger AI SDLC pipeline — DicomReceiverThread.java fix
Hand off to Jenkins — CHG-8821 created and queued for deployment

scenarios.title: SAP Batch Job Recovery — Finance & Procurement
scenarios.subtitle: INC0002103 · SAP ERP Azure · overnight batch stalled · downstream blocked
scenarios.blastRadius: Invoice generation · Procurement approval · Financial ledger feed · Month-end reporting
scenarios.reversibility: HIGH — job reschedule fully reversible · no data loss · idempotent restart

outcome.result: SAP batch cleared — 847 documents posted — finance & procurement restored

steps.label: Alert Correlation
steps.summary: SAP batch queue depth anomaly detected — 847 documents stalled · INC0002103 raised

events.msg: Prometheus: sap_batch_queue_depth > 500 · threshold breached at 02:14
Datadog: SAP work process utilisation 94% · dialog pool saturated
Correlating 23 alerts → 1 incident · blast radius: finance + procurement
INC0002103 raised — P1 · SAP ERP Azure · 847 documents in queue

steps.label: Log Analysis
steps.summary: SM37 job log analysis — RFBIBL00 document posting job identified as stall point

events.msg: Querying SAP application server logs — SM37 batch job history last 4h
Job RFBIBL00 (FI document posting) — status ACTIVE since 01:47 · 27 min no progress
Lock contention detected — BKPF/BSEG tables held by dialog process WP-04
Downstream jobs queued: RFDABL00 (AR), RFEBBU00 (bank statement), MM60 (procurement)
Root cause confirmed: dialog work process lock blocking background job

steps.label: Root Cause Analysis
steps.summary: Dialog work process WP-04 holding BKPF table lock · 27 minutes · job cascade identified

events.msg: Traversing SAP dependency graph — batch job → work process → table lock chain
WP-04 lock age: 27 min 14 sec · origin: FI posting session user BATCH_FI
Cascade identified: RFBIBL00 → RFDABL00 → RFEBBU00 → MM60 all blocked
Pattern match: similar to INC0001987 (6 weeks ago) · same WP lock pattern · 91% confidence
Risk score: 17/100 · Sentinel approved · proceeding with autonomous remediation

steps.label: Work Process Release
steps.summary: Stale dialog session cancelled · BKPF lock released · batch queue begins draining

events.msg: Connecting to SAP via RFC — SM04 session manager
Terminating stale dialog session BATCH_FI WP-04 — lock age 27 min · safe to cancel
Session cancelled · BKPF/BSEG table locks released · WP-04 free
RFBIBL00 job resuming · document posting restarted · queue draining
Work process pool: 94% → 41% utilisation · dialog and background balanced

steps.label: Downstream Validation
steps.summary: All 4 downstream jobs released · 847 documents processed · finance and procurement flows restored

events.msg: Monitoring downstream job cascade — RFDABL00, RFEBBU00, MM60 restarting
RFDABL00 (AR posting): ACTIVE · processing 312 open items
RFEBBU00 (bank statement): ACTIVE · 23 statements queued for import
MM60 (procurement): ACTIVE · 47 purchase orders releasing for approval
847 FI documents posted · invoice generation unblocked · ledger feed flowing
All downstream processes confirmed healthy — data flowing end-to-end

steps.label: Prevention & Close
steps.summary: Preventive monitoring rule added · INC0002103 auto-closed · KEDB updated

events.msg: Validating SAP financial calendar — month-end close unaffected · 08:00 deadline safe
Adding SM04 lock age alert: trigger at 10 min (was unmonitored) · prevents recurrence
KEDB updated — WP lock pattern KB-AG-SAP-001 created
INC0002103 auto-closed in ServiceNow · MTTR: 22m 14s · P1 SLA met
